candidates.direction_name: Recover a compact correctness-safe 4-row PTX writer sweep

score_breakdown.reason: Best next move because it directly tests whether the round-7 low-register signal can survive a better full-row writer shape.

ranking_notes: Round 7 is still worth mining because it doubled active warps and cut registers drastically.
Round 8 falsified only the naive correctness repair shape, not the overall value of a tighter writer.
Keep this bounded to the PTX export sweep; do not mix in barrier or geometry changes.
candidates.hypothesis: The round-8 repair showed that simply appending TileRow 2 and 3 restores correctness but also restores the old 198-register anchor. The next meaningful variant is to keep the new per-tile export helper shape but make the full 4-row sweep more compact than the current explicit 0/1/2/3 expansion, for example through a tighter compile-time row walker or paired-row emission that still writes every row exactly once.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: occupancy_latency_hiding_issue in the PTX export/store path if a compact full-row sweep can retain some of the low-register behavior while staying correct
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if a compact full-row sweep still snaps back to the same 198-register profile within noise, or if correctness fails again.

target_hotspots.explanation: The direct correctness repair returned to 198 registers, which is exactly the regression this direction tries to avoid.
The target is to recover at least part of the lost active-warps gain while staying correct.

expected_local_changes: Keep full 4-row correctness, but replace the current direct 0/1/2/3 expansion with a more compact row sweep.
Preserve the new per-tile export helper and late address arithmetic shape if possible.
Avoid mixing this with barrier retiming or geometry changes.

guardrail_metrics.reason: The compact writer is only meaningful if it stays correct.
A writer-side refinement should not get worse than the current correct anchor.

candidates.direction_name: Retime the barrier seam on the current correct 128x128 PTX anchor

score_breakdown.reason: This is still the best bounded follow-on if the writer-side compact sweep does not materialize.

ranking_notes: Barrier remains real on the current correct anchor, but it is not the first thing to test while the round-7 speed signal is still partially unexplained.
Use this if dir_01 cannot retain any occupancy win while staying correct.
candidates.hypothesis: If the writer surface cannot stay compact while correct, the best remaining bounded exploit on the current correct anchor is still the wait-group and CTA barrier seam. The live profile is back to the familiar 198-register / 16.63%-active-warps regime with 5.96% barrier and 6.8% long-scoreboard, so the barrier handoff remains the next local seam.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: synchronization_barrier_issue layered on top of occupancy_latency_hiding_issue in the current correctness-safe PTX anchor
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if barrier retiming grows shared memory, breaks correctness, or improves barrier without any runtime win.

target_hotspots.explanation: Barrier remains the cleanest local seam on the current correct anchor.

expected_local_changes: Retime the cp.async wait-group and CTA sync seam only on the current correct PTX path.
Keep the writer semantics and 22016-byte shared-memory footprint fixed.
Do not mix barrier work with a writer sweep rewrite.

guardrail_metrics.reason: Any barrier follow-up must stay on a correctness-safe surface.
Do not just swap barrier for scoreboard stalls.

candidates.direction_name: Fallback to the current correctness-safe PTX writer anchor if compact sweeping stalls out

score_breakdown.reason: Necessary guardrail fallback if the compact writer sweep still cannot retain the low-register signal.

ranking_notes: This is a fallback, not the main recommendation.
Select it only if dir_01 proves that the round-7 speedup cannot survive a correctness-safe full-row writer.
Keep workflow and workload support files on HEAD if any implementation restore is used.
candidates.hypothesis: If the compact full-row writer sweep cannot preserve any of the round-7 signal, the search should fall back to the current correctness-safe writer anchor and stop spending budget on this local family for a while. That keeps the loop disciplined instead of repeatedly re-learning the same writer-side loss.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: fallback recovery to the current correctness-safe PTX writer anchor
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if this restore is chosen before the compact full-row writer sweep gets a fair attempt, unless new evidence shows the signal was entirely due to missing work.

target_hotspots.explanation: This restore is the safety net if the compact writer sweep still cannot improve the current correct anchor.

expected_local_changes: If needed, return to the known correct writer semantics from b79a9bf.
Preserve workflow/workload support files on HEAD.
Treat this as a guardrail recovery, not as the preferred search move.

guardrail_metrics.reason: The fallback exists to preserve a correctness-safe anchor.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
